body:
GOP Senator Dean Heller thought he could flip his vote on Obamacare repeal -- and get away with it. Show him just how wrong he is:

**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170725-Rapid-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**

Team,

Weeks ago, Republican Senator Dean Heller announced that he wouldn't support the GOP bill to repeal Obamacare. (And tried to get as much press as he could for it, too!)

But then, a Trump-aligned super PAC started running ads in Nevada pressuring him to vote for the bill. And just this past week, Trump sat next to him at lunch and threatened him, saying, "He wants to remain a senator, doesn't he?"

Today, Heller voted to advance Obamacare repeal, choosing Trump and his billionaire allies over health care for his constituents.

[**Let 's show Dean Heller that he's not going to get away with placing politics ahead of what's right for his state. We just launched new ads calling out Heller's actions on health care -- can we count on you to chip in $1 now to help us hold him and the rest of the Republicans accountable in 2018?**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170725-Rapid-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Heller's vote today was a vote to force his constituents to pay more for less care, to drive up premiums by 20 percent, to impose an age tax on older Americans and to strip away coverage for pre-existing conditions.

**With his vote, Heller sealed his fate as another desperate, self-serving Washington politician too interested in helping himself to stand up for the people who elected him.**

Now it's up to us to make sure this vote will be one of his last.

[**Chip in $1 now to help fund our ads highlighting Heller 's disgusting record on health care and all of our efforts to replace the Republican majority in 2018.**](https://secure.dscc. name: The White House
subject: A Message from President Trump on Obamacare
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d747585f41c7fef5d8937ef166ddcabfb73eca9447a8dfefaebf9296f6b8a2a1bbc31dc1e7081b33401a956b6ad73ca6f4b934

A Statement From The President

"I applaud the Senate for taking a giant step to end the Obamacare nightmare. As this vote shows, inaction is not an option, and now the legislative process can move forward as intended to produce a bill that lowers costs and increases options for all Americans.
The Senate must now pass a bill and get it to my desk so we can finally end the Obamacare disaster once and for all."

-President Donald J. Trump

-------------

President Trump Salutes American Heroes in Ohio
Yesterday evening, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump traveled to Struthers, Ohio to participate in a special Salute to American Heroes. At the event, President Trump made remarks acknowledging the heroes who put the lives of other Americans before their own every day.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d747589b5ea7d5cc041ad99188b589fdda680186560ccf39814453346146d07ce67b2eae511cef540d9eba75ba59c76470656f
Watch the Video

Yesterday, President Trump met with Prime Minister Hariri of Lebanon
The President and Prime Minister discussed topics such as the importance of defeating terrorist organizations like ISIS and Al Qaeda. The President commended the Lebanese people for “standing up for humanity in a very troubled part of the world.” The meeting aimed to strengthen the two country's relationship in order to ensure stability, mutual prosperity, and peace.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d7475830ed432aec9bc5e032f4b772f4401a624b41c9c444a76857cf350e87461292e7644dece64303973f1556e528f200b427
Watch the Joint Press Conference

White House Hosts Second Regional Media Day
On Tuesday, the White House hosted its second Regional Media Day. There were 95 interviews broadcast on television and radio from the North Lawn. The interviews were given by Senior White House Officials and Cabinet members, and were focused on the priorities of the Trump Administration, such as the push to repeal and replace Obamacare as well as tax reform.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d747585d786ca25d03812770d06127bf3d3118b657065f59331dbd1c6b78aa46aaab1a7be9fd091166004b111350671caa4682
View Photos

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump honors veteran Bob Bishop at the AMVETS Post 44 Salute to American Heroes

| July 25, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump and Vice President Pence will participate in an American Legion event at the White House. The President and Vice President will also make a jobs announcement from the East Room.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d74758bfdd8c3ff36a43cafeea0225a015ebc8066aafb81f22bc272a69ac97eb60631c607d14fcc103284a6af1909c8f92db39
Watch the announcement live at 5pm EST.

Coming Up This Week

This week, the White House continues to celebrate and honor American Heroes. Tomorrow, President Trump will welcome the brave first responders from Alexandria, Virginia to the White House, and on Friday, the President will travel to New York to meet with law enforcement on Long Island.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d747580672bbd06d83acb5b9bd414d739fddbb27ce76ca23ee0ed6ae896808f8c2e77be662204d7068d2c592bdd4551d522075
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2acef71d01d747582f0376e12f8e7869b66eff7c1693b92a5674c82234ddf830ba34caa7b7af9cb4d24884a2ae49dd41b5bb4e5b37d42e5f
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4fddba34fa70806c79585754062791d319a7b1d514de0cfcb6f7700a16ecf068b9a2c8c82456c098e55bc5ff582535989
Unsubscribe

body:
Call on Congress to take action on pro-growth tax reform

| | | | Peter,

I need your help!

Virginia activists recently helped members of the Prince William County Board of Supervisors tentatively ward off a bad stadium deal that would've cost taxpayers upwards of $35 million.

**This is a step in the right direction, but it's time to oppose stadiums subsidies altogether.**

Americans for Prosperity-Virginia is excited to announce its support of a [ recently proposed resolution ](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=ad0b2e0669c7fda4aab02840d8aa60c5e07cc115ea91a9264711a72d9a732b11f65e5d678e3cdc794f51987b71bf845a031898bdb2237c1d) aiming to end public subsidies.

This resolution to end stadium subsidies would prohibit all taxpayer financing of professional sports stadiums once a majority of states have passed similar legislation.

It's time for Virginia to take the next step.

**[Sign the petition today](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=1b5e38e0892bf78bd07f1137261955f14296b9a81b477379a7da7836c9481fe6b32ac2d73eedadf4ec2210dc3356c2950698a335df2af131)** urging lawmakers to stand up for Virginians and oppose stadium subsidies for good.
For Freedom,

JC Hernandez
State Director
Americans for Prosperity-Virginia

name: Bill Derrough
subject: A disgraceful, cowardly attack on American patriots
body:
This ban must be reversed
---
**All those who would defend our right to live freely should be able to serve freely. Add your name: tell Donald Trump to reverse his ban on transgender Americans serving in the military.
**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405231b8/4067b0a/31f73eb41/7e6de822/2909504112/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZtYVhKemRHNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IldpbGxpcyIsIkpTVmxiV0ZwYkNVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMjAxMTIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/Reverse-the-Ban1?firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170726_bd_s3&source=em_20170726_bd_s3")**
**
Derek --

My father enlisted in the Navy in 1942, just a few months after Pearl Harbor. He served for six years, earning numerous service awards and commendations before he was honorably discharged.

I always knew I wanted to follow in his footsteps and serve my country the same way he did. I went to college on a Navy ROTC scholarship, but because of the ban on gays serving in the military, in my senior year I was unable to continue and receive my commission as a U.S. Naval officer and fulfill my dream of serving my country.

We have come a long way since then, and, thanks to President Obama, hundreds of thousands of LGBTQ Americans have finally been able to proudly, openly serve in our armed forces.

**[But today, Donald Trump turned back the clock and will ban transgender people from serving in the military. He must reverse course -- join me and sign your name if you agree.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405231b8/4067b0a/31f73eb41/7e6de823/2909504112/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZtYVhKemRHNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IldpbGxpcyIsIkpTVmxiV0ZwYkNVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMjAxMTIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/Reverse-the-Ban1?firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+But+today+Donald+Trump+turned+back+the+c&utm_campaign=em_20170726_bd_s3&source=em_20170726_bd_s3")**

Right now, there are thousands of transgender Americans on active duty and in the reserves. They protect all of us -- including Donald Trump and Mike Pence -- from harm.

So let's be clear: what Donald Trump did this morning was a disgraceful attack on thousands of patriots by a coward. It shames our nation and the progress we have made advancing diversity in the military, and it is an insult to the millions of service members who have courageously served their country.

Democrats stand with the transgender community, and we will fight Donald Trump's bigotry tooth-and-nail.

**Tell Trump to reverse this ban and allow all those who would defend our right to live freely to serve freely. Add your name with mine:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405231b8/4067b0a/31f73eb41/7e6de820/2909504112/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZtYVhKemRHNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IldpbGxpcyIsIkpTVmxiV0ZwYkNVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMjAxMTIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/Reverse-the-Ban1?firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170726_bd_s3&source=em_20170726_bd_s3")**
Thank you,

Bill

Bill Derrough
Treasurer
Democratic National Committee

Derek,

I have some bad news. Despite extraordinary grassroots efforts, Senate Republicans voted to begin the process of taking health care away from tens of millions of Americans.

I'm extremely worried on behalf of my constituents in Virginia and for all Americans. As the wealthiest and most compassionate nation in the world, it's our obligation to make our health care system better for American families -- not worse.

name: The White House
subject: The Rust Belt Gave President Trump a Hero’s Welcome
body:
1600 Daily
"Why the Rust Belt just gave Donald Trump a hero’s welcome"

- Salena Zito, in the New York Post

Last night in Youngstown, Ohio, the “town was on fire,” writes Salena Zito, in the New York Post. Preparing for the President’s visit, “By 1 in the afternoon on Tuesday, every main thoroughfare downtown was filled with happy people heading toward the Covelli Centre,” Zito reported. She interviewed Donald Skowron, a retired police officer, who said he was “very happy with the president’s performance so far,” and Dave Torrance who said the president is “doing the right things.” She offers insights into how the President connects with working class voters, and how he is offering them hope that once again, the American dream is alive.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c548af8d34804893dbb9983ac20a363a79a2ca7dc8b1c4edd203dabf203383e3b4d232a381087c2f761b922d9b0530c7316b
Click here to read more.

------------

In
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c5488750ac5c1a3692d354e4e362c20aa55d0b1a3069d1e1f7a25072705b3680362cc48bdf41aca8a5c653d99ae26820eb8d
The Daily Signal , Walt Heyer writes “I was once transgender. Why I think Trump made the right decision for the military,” writing the government “paying for transition-related surgeries for military service members and their families is beyond comprehensible.”

------------

On the healthcare front, in
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c548b0684dfd0049467b8adac0e57c5ccdae899a41c887080d107c53eface1a6edc3afa172d6af78ac20e767397e4bc8659c
USA Today, Sen. Tim Scott writes that "families across the country are seeing triple-digit premium hikes and sky-high deductibles” under Obamacare, and the need for the Senate to act and pass legislation repealing and replacing the failed law is needed “to ensure that every American has access to quality, affordable care.”

------------

In incredible economic news,
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c5483861bbc6ee19c9e5b41c575e5782a1e9fcb5383d8849f44c00a1db20186ffdf8a8e79d1f7588a2ce466e7975a997966f
Reuters reports that according to data from the Conference Board, “U.S. consumer confidence jumped to a near 16-year high in July amid optimism over the labor market,” a report that “underscore[s] the economy’s strong fundamentals.”

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c54804418a58b9ab8b450eb83f1756917608cee21cc12dc774d1b0a0631286b054047fdd16691f359edf700a6506b3a89217
CNBC reports that both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq hit “intraday and closing” record highs on Tuesday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c548457be82ffbae73b30cdfb80126bf9f5155fa48fe1cfc1b81de19c0a1fae11036224359779274cf82991be48b9478aa53
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=27b50d630846c548e7590a923dcabad0bf46a87ae2d8449b76d9a48b61396e0172f4254244dd832e096240026c1cb51de1ddfa7f8d1d86de
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4fddba34fa70806c76fa41a4364002053d1d3647631dffa77132cddbfe7ead852274f3f17af2792a06d59663acd1aa426
Unsubscribe

We've got the perfect candidate ready to take his seat: Jacky Rosen. She's a champion for women and families, and she has a real shot at booting Heller from the Senate once and for all.

We've already endorsed Jacky Rosen to take on and defeat Heller. Now we have to make sure she has the resources to win.

name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Veterans Choice Program
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: July 26, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Carol Shea-Porter
and Annie Kuster voting against the Veterans Choice Program

CONCORD- NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following
statement regarding Congresswomen Carol Shea-Porter’s and Annie
Kuster’s failure to support the Veterans Choice Program on
Tuesday. The program is used extensively by the Manchester VA
Medical Center and is about to run out of money. New Hampshire’s
two representatives were among those who opposed the emergency
funding measure.

“At a time when the resources of the Manchester VA are under well
known scrutiny, the votes from Carol Shea-Porter and Annie Kuster
are extremely disappointing. New Hampshire veterans deserve the
funding to get the best medical care they need, and through the
Veterans Choice Program they were able to. Failing to fund an
extension of the program is an embarrassment for New Hampshire’s
representation in Congress.”

“New Hampshire deserves representation in Congress that
understands and fights for the critical funding programs our
veterans need. Carol Shea-Porter and Annie Kuster should be
ashamed of their feckless votes against ensuring our veterans get
the choices in health care they deserve.”

###

body:
We have to hold Republicans accountable for this.
---
**Senate Republicans voted yesterday to repeal our health care -- so we're going to vote them out of office. Help us raise $100,000 to elect Democrats today, and get your donation matched.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405231cd/4067b0a/31d4a35c3/7e6dd475/2275039348/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Accountability-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170726_joc_nd&source=em_20170726_joc_nd&refcode=em_20170726_joc_nd")**
**
Derek --

Yesterday, 50 Senate Republicans and Mike Pence voted to start the process of taking millions of Americans' health care away -- without any idea of what comes next.

I don't know what kind of people would vote to leave parents, seniors, veterans, pregnant women, and people with chronic illnesses in the dark, uncertain if they'll have access to lifesaving care when they need it. I don't know what kind of people would push a partisan political agenda at the expense of the most vulnerable Americans. I don't know what kind of elected official would so flagrantly ignore the will of the people they are supposed to represent.

But I do know that none of those people belong in Congress. **[So we're going to fight like hell to throw them out.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405231cd/4067b0a/31d4a35c3/7e6dd472/2275039348/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Accountability-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+So+were+going+to+fight+like+hell+to+thro&utm_campaign=em_20170726_joc_nd&source=em_20170726_joc_nd&refcode=em_20170726_joc_nd")**

We launched the Health Care Accountability Fund to make sure Republicans pay the price for voting for repeal. We're going to ramp up our electoral efforts in their states and talk to every single voter about the vote to repeal. And we won't rest until we've elected Democrats from every corner of the country who will fight to protect our care.

If you're with us, pitch in $3 or whatever you can right now to our Health Care Accountability Fund. For a limited time, a group of generous donors will match every contribution dollar-for-dollar so you can double your impact.

Senate Republicans don't exactly know how they're going to rip health care away from tens of millions of Americans -- but they voted for it anyway.

This vote isn't just irresponsible. It isn't just reckless. It isn't just cruel.

This vote is immoral. It goes against everything we stand for in this country -- and everything we stand for as Americans.

But this fight isn't over -- not by a long shot. We still have time to stop the final passage of this bill. We can still stop the Republicans from gutting Medicaid and taking away millions of Americans' health care so that America's richest families can get a tax break.

This is the week we must prove: the Senate doesn't just work for the billionaires and giant corporations, it doesn't just work for the lawyers and lobbyists, and it doesn't just work for one bought-and-paid-for political party -- it works for the people.

Speak out, make your voice heard, and persist.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: My birthday
body:
#
Team --
Thirty-eight years ago today, I was born to my mother Frances, a bus driver, and my daddy Charles, a construction worker in Miami, Florida.
They didn't know that their son would go on to become the youngest City Commissioner in Tallahassee's history. Or that he'd later become Mayor. Or that he'd run for Governor.
All they knew was that they loved me, and they wanted to give me the best opportunities they could. They didn't give me a famous last name or millions of dollars in wealth. They gave me love -- and a chance.
Today, I'm running to become Governor of Florida to honor what my parents gave me on my first birthday -- a chance to do well and get ahead.
And I've tried to take that chance whenever it's presented itself.
When I was in the 7th grade, I didn't think I belonged in honors classes at Westwood Middle School in Gainesville. My language arts teacher, Ms. Alexandria, encouraged me to sign up for them, but I wasn't sure I belonged. None of my older siblings had ever graduated from high school, let alone taken an honors class. But she insisted -- and ultimately persisted -- in getting me to take that chance.
When I first ran for City Commission, the cynics and critics told me I was too young and too inexperienced. That I needed to wait my turn and one day they might decide it was my turn. But all I asked for in that campaign was a chance -- and the wonderful people of Tallahassee granted me one.
And today -- on my birthday -- all I'm asking for is a chance.
A chance to help change Florida for the better. A chance to help working families in every corner of our state make ends meet. A chance for every person to have access to a job with dignity. A chance for every person to have access to affordable and quality healthcare as a right. A chance to help every student get a high-quality public education.
[**Will you help give me this chance to serve the state of Florida by chipping in to our campaign for Governor today?**](https://gillum.bsd.net/page/m/fcb32ce/9e00690/4c084781/2403636c/3434681333/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-july2017?refcode=em20170726-38")
As we continue on this journey, I hope you'll join me so we can give every Floridian the opportunity they need to succeed and live a fulfilled life -- no matter your race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, class, age, ability, or background.
So as I celebrate my birthday today, I want you to help me give every Floridian what I got on my first birthday -- a real chance.
Bring it home,
Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://gillum.bsd.net/page/m/fcb32ce/9e00690/4c084781/2403636c/3434681333/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-july2017?refcode=em20170726-38")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://gillum.bsd.net/page/m/fcb32ce/9e00690/4c084781/2403636e/3434681333/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: what we're up against
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Friend,

Yesterday, Senate Republicans took the first step on a very dangerous path that could blow up much of our health care system. In essence, the passage of the 'Motion to Proceed' opens the door for Mitch McConnell to start the bidding war to get the Republican votes he needs to pass some kind of Trumpcare legislation and begin conferencing with House Republicans (read Freedom Caucus) on a final bill.

Health care is serious business and I don't want to sugarcoat my feelings. The callousness displayed by this process is stunning. Senators were asked to vote yesterday to give Senator McConnell a blank check to bring up for a vote -- without prior notice -- proposals cooked up in secret behind closed doors that would do grave harm to our entire health care system.

This is no way to address an issue as critical to the lives of tens of millions of Americans as healthcare. It makes you sick to your stomach. It also proves once again that this exercise is not about health care at all -- it's about giving big corporations and the wealthiest Americans a big tax cut.

The good news is that today, Republicans lost the vote on full Obamacare repeal. [**But the votes in the next 48 hours will determine the final outcome.**](https://cvanhollen.bsd.net/page/m/2de91bcf/70cdc2ba/4686c195/397c3b2/1416188295/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-help-us-defeat-the-republican-health-care-bill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+But+the+votes+in+the+next+48+hours+will+&utm_campaign=em072617&source=em072617")

While we don't know exactly which bill will be presented for a final vote, we do know that all of the options would be catastrophic for the American people. Depending on the plan anywhere from 20 to 30 million Americans will be at risk of losing access to health care.

[**I hope you will stand with me and my Senate Democratic colleagues against this unconscionable attempt to pass Wealthcare on the backs of hardworking Americans.**](https://cvanhollen.bsd.net/page/m/2de91bcf/70cdc2ba/4686c195/397c3b3/1416188295/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-help-us-defeat-the-republican-health-care-bill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+I+hope+you+will+stand+with+me+and+my+Sen&utm_campaign=em072617&source=em072617")

One thing's for sure: we will keep fighting tooth and nail for a country where every American has access to the health care they need.

Your voices count. Thank you for being part of this,

Chris

---

---

subject: the deciding vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fdec3/2a380d31/1ef5dbb4/fe4103/396049616/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fdec3/2a380d31/1ef5dbb4/fe4103/396049616/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Friends,
Yesterday, Senate Republicans voted to move forward with repealing the Affordable Care Act and advance Trumpcare by just ONE vote.
While we need to hold every Republican who voted for this cruel, immoral measure accountable, Nevada's Dean Heller is widely seen as casting the deciding vote.
He's the most vulnerable Republican in the Senate, and we have just the person to defeat him: Jacky Rosen.
**Jacky is committed to making sure that no one goes without the care they need, but she can 't win without our help. [If you're outraged about Dean Heller's vote (and you should be!), give $3 or more to Jacky's campaign to defeat Heller and elect a champion for hard-working families.](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fdec3/2a380d31/1ef5dbb4/fe410c/396049616/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxN19td2g0anIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDcyNl9NV0g0SlItSGVhbHRoX2FjdCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2017_mwh4jr?refcode=e20170726_MWH4JR-Health_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**

But after Trump-aligned outside groups ran ads pressuring him to vote for the bill and Donald Trump publicly threatened his job, Heller put corporate special interests ahead of hard-working Americans and voted to move the GOP's disastrous plan forward.
Heller's corporate special interests are going to throw the kitchen sink at this race and pull out all of the stops to reelect him, so we need everyone on this team to commit to electing Jacky - including you, please.
[**Donate $3 or whatever you can to Jacky 's campaign and hold Heller accountable for his cowardly, life-threatening vote to move forward with ripping health care coverage away from those who need it most.**](https://hassan.bsd.net/page/m/f7fdec3/2a380d31/1ef5dbb4/fe410c/396049616/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxN19td2g0anIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDcyNl9NV0g0SlItSGVhbHRoX2FjdCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2017_mwh4jr?refcode=e20170726_MWH4JR-Health_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
